name: Bharatanatyam
description: One of the oldest classical dance forms of India, originating from Tamil Nadu. Known for its fixed upper torso, bent legs, and intricate facial expressions.

name: Kathak
description: A classical dance form from North India, known for its storytelling aspect, intricate footwork, and swift spins.

name: Kuchipudi
description: A classical dance form from Andhra Pradesh, combining fast rhythms, fluid movements, and expressive gestures.

name: Odissi
description: An ancient classical dance form from Odisha, characterized by its tribhangi posture and graceful movements.

name: Manipuri
description: A graceful and gentle dance form from Manipur, featuring smooth, flowing movements and circular choreography.

name: Kathakali
description: A highly stylized classical dance form from Kerala, known for its elaborate costumes, makeup, and dramatic expressions.

name: Mohiniyattam
description: A graceful classical dance form from Kerala, characterized by gentle, swaying movements and elegant gestures.

name: Sattriya
description: A classical dance form from Assam, traditionally performed in monasteries as part of religious practices.
